title: Brazil 1-1 Morocco Stats: Late Alisson Double Save Preserves Point After Vinicius Stunner | Opta Analyst
fetched: raw-HTML 直抓 + 抽取(apify:website-content-crawler) + 清洗（逐字、未经任何模型/改写/翻译）
original_text:
Brazil 1-1 Morocco Stats: Late Alisson Double Save Preserves Point After Vinicius Stunner
Ismael Saibari and Vinicius Junior traded first-half goals, but it was a late Alisson intervention that ensured the points were shared after a cautious second half from both Brazil and Morocco.
Vinícius Júnior and Alisson ensured Brazil earned a point in their World Cup opener, as they came from behind to draw 1-1 with Morocco.
The Atlas Lions got their reward for a stellar start when Ismael Saibari punished poor defending, but Vinicius, along with Alisson’s late heroics, ensured Carlo Ancelotti’s first World Cup match did not end in defeat.
Morocco took the game to Brazil from the first whistle, leading the shot count 5-1 within the opening 10 minutes, though none of those tested Alisson.
But in the 21st minute, Ancelotti’s side were cut wide open. Brahim Diaz slid a lovely throughball into Saibari, who had acres of space between the centre-backs to lift a lovely chip over Alisson, who rushed out of his box.
Achraf Hakimi almost punished Brazil’s lax defending a second time shortly after, but his low effort fizzed just wide of the far post.
Vinicius dragged his team back onto level terms in the 32nd minute, though, cutting inside to whip a thunderous shot past a helpless Yassine Bounou to mark his 50th Brazil appearance with a stunning goal.
Lucas Paqueta could have put Brazil in front before the break, meeting a left-sided cross with an acrobatic volley, but Bounou was alert to push the effort away.
A more subdued second half saw Brazil up the pressure late on without causing too many real problems, as Mohamed Ouahbi’s side defended resolutely.
And it was Morocco who almost forced a late winner, but Alisson got down sharply to make a brilliant double save to keep out Neil El Aynaoui from distance and Ayoube Amaimouni-Echghouyab on the follow-up.
Morocco Show Credentials as Brazil Fall Short of Comeback
Brazil are a daunting prospect in the early stages of the World Cup, having topped their first-round group in every World Cup since 1982, and now, they have not lost their opening match since 1934 (against Spain, W17 D4 since).
However, Morocco showed no signs of fear as they recorded the first four attempts of the game, while it was only the second time in the tournament’s history that Brazil have faced five shots in the first 10 minutes of a game (since 1966).
In fact, they had 12 shots within the opening 30 minutes, more than the Selecao had conceded in any of their previous six full World Cup matches, but after that, the chances dried up for Morocco. In fact, they only managed two more, with both of those coming in the 99th minute.
Vinicius more than played his part too, scoring his 10th goal for Brazil, with three of those coming on United States soil. However, his 100% winning record when scoring (eight prior to this match) has now ended.
He has certainly made his case as the leading man, with Neymar still not fit enough to play, but Ancelotti has work to do as Morocco proved they can threaten for top spot.
Our Opta match centre delivers you all the Brazil vs Morocco stats from their 2026 FIFA World Cup Group C meeting at the New York New Jersey Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey on 13 June.
The match centre below includes team and player stats, expected goals data, passing networks, an Opta chalkboard and more. It gives you everything you need to do your own match analysis.
Underneath the match centre you can find the official Opta stats on the game as well.
Brazil vs Morocco: Selected Post-Match Opta Facts
Brazil are unbeaten in their opening match in the last 21 FIFA World Cups dating back to 1938 (W17 D4). It’s the longest such streak in the history of the FIFA World Cup, more than twice as long as any other (Germany, Netherlands – 9 straight).
Morocco recorded their first point and scored their first goal against a South American side in the FIFA World Cup (previously 0-3 losses to Peru in 1970 and Brazil in 1998).
Morocco are unbeaten in their last five FIFA World Cup group stage matches (W2 D3), marking their longest such run in this of the tournament, surpassing their previous record of four games (W1 D3) between 1970 and 1986.
Morocco attempted 12 shots in the first half against Brazil but failed to register an attempt after the break until the ninth minute of stoppage time.
Morocco are only the second side on record (since 1966) to attempt five shots against Brazil within the opening 10 minutes of a FIFA World Cup match, joining Portugal who achieved the feat in 1966.
Achraf Hakimi made his 11th appearance for Morocco at the FIFA World Cup, equalling the most by an African player in the competition’s history, level with Cameroon’s François Omam-Biyik and Ghana’s Asamoah Gyan.
Vinícius Júnior scored his 10th goal for Brazil, with three of those coming on US soil – only scoring more in Brazil (4). Brazil had won all eight of the previous matches in which Vinícius Júnior scored, drawing for the first time.
In addition to scoring the opening goal for Morocco against Brazil, Ismael Saibari applied 100 pressures, equalling the most by any player in a single match at the 2026 FIFA World Cup (with Paraguay’s Andrés Cubas 100).
